disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Small itchy blisters in tight clusters appeared on both elbows and the buttocks over about 24 hours
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the rash and what does it look like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's little blisters in clusters on both my elbows and my buttocks. They came up over about a day and they're incredibly itchy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The eruption is symmetrical and on both sides equally, over the pointy/extensor parts of the elbows and buttocks
trigger_question_examples: Is it on one side only or both?
Is it in a band or line, or spread evenly on both sides?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's on both elbows the same and on both sides of my backside — it's pretty even, not just one spot or one side.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The itch is severe, relentless, and worse at night, causing a full night of lost sleep
trigger_question_examples: How bad is the itch and does it affect your sleep?
Is it worse at any time of day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's unbearable and much worse at night — I was up the entire night scratching and couldn't sleep at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No one else in the shared household has a similar rash
trigger_question_examples: Does anyone you live with have the same itchy rash?
Have close contacts developed anything similar?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nobody else in the house has it — it's just me so far, which is part of why I'm confused and scared.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new topical products, soaps, plants, or novel skin exposures were started before the rash
trigger_question_examples: Have you started any new soap, cream, plant, or chemical recently?
Anything new touching your skin in the last few days?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't changed soaps or used any new creams or been around anything new that I can think of.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Some scratched areas are raw with pinpoint crusts, but there is no fever and no yellow pus or spreading heat/redness
trigger_question_examples: Are any of the scratched areas hot, swollen, or draining pus?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Some spots are raw and scabbed from scratching, but there's no pus and I don't have a fever — the skin doesn't feel hot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient fears the rash is a contagious infestation (scabies) or shingles and is anxious about household transmission
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried this could be?
Is there a particular fear driving your concern today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm terrified it's scabies or something contagious and that I'll give it to everyone in the house. Please tell me what it is.
